channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Apple va lancer une mise à jour d’urgence sur certains iPhone, êtes-vous concerné ?
item.description: Apple s’apprête à mettre à jour d'urgence 20 % des iPhone en circulation. Le géant californien cherche à protéger ses clients contre les pirates russes qui exploitent le kit de piratage DarkSword.

item.title: Claude Code leak suggests Anthropic is working on a 'Proactive' mode for its coding tool
item.description: What should have been a routine release has revealed some of the features Anthropic has been working on for Claude Code. As reported by Ars Technica, The Verge and others, after the company released Claude Code's 2.1.88 update on Tuesday, users found it contained a file that exposed the app's source code. Before Anthropic took action to plug the leak, the codebase was uploaded to a public GitHub repository, where it was subsequently copied more than 50,000 times. All told, the entire internet (and Anthropic's competitors) got a chance to examine more than 512,000 lines of code and 2,000 TypeSc...

item.title: 72 New Sci-Fi, Fantasy, and Horror Books Arriving in April
item.description: Patrick Ness ('Chaos Walking') and James S.A. Corey ('The Expanse' series) are among the authors releasing new titles this month.

item.title: A Cosmic ‘Dead Zone’ for Black Holes Is Real, New Evidence Suggests
item.description: LIGO dropped a ton of new data, and astronomers are having a field day.

item.title: The New Era of Militia Influencers
item.description: The militia movement in the US is undergoing a rebrand that trades counterprotests for expensive weapons, manly sweatshirts, and highly curated Instagram grids.

item.title: Ventes Tesla France : record historique en mars 2026 avec le Model Y
item.description: Tesla vient de réaliser le meilleur mois de son histoire en France. Avec 13 945 unités au premier trimestre, le constructeur américain a repris des couleurs. Le Model Y repousse la Renault 5 E-Tech au second plan.

item.title: The AI Doc's director was "scared shitless" by AI, so he made a movie about it
item.description: If you're feeling anxious about AI and what it means for the future of humanity, you should watch The AI Doc: Or, How I Became an Apocaloptimist. As I noted in my review, the film aims to deliver some clarity amid all the hype. Now that it's in theaters, we sat down with director Daniel Roher, who won an Oscar for his film Navalny, to dive deeper into his complicated feelings around AI.The entire topic made him nervous, Roher said, so he decided to team up with similarly anxious colleagues to demystify AI using film. He describes the goal of the project to be a sort of "first date" with AI, a ...

item.title: Amazon brade à un super prix ce pack Nothing incluant le Phone (3a) Pro et le Headphone (1)
item.description: Amazon propose un pack avec deux produits Nothing notés 8 et 9/10, le smartphone Nothing Phone (3a) Pro et le Nothing Headphone (1), pour 613,82 euros au lieu de 778 euros.

item.title: Nothing Might Get Into Smart Glasses at the Worst Possible Time
item.description: Even with an eye-catching design, privacy concerns over smart glasses could make them DOA.

item.title: Hasbro says it was hacked, and may take ‘several weeks’ to recover
item.description: The American toymaking giant noted that it was continuing to "implement measures to secure its business operations," suggesting that the hackers may still be in the company's systems.

item.title: Looking for a job? It could be a scam — NordVPN uncovers phishing campaign impersonating top brands' recruiters
item.description: Cybercriminals are impersonating top brands like Meta, Disney, and Spotify in a highly sophisticated new phishing campaign designed to hijack your Facebook account. Here is everything you need to know to stay safe.

item.title: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale 2026: all of the latest deals
item.description: If you were looking for the best time to get a deal this spring, it might have just passed you by, as Amazon’s Big Spring Sale is over. That being said, several deals are still happening (some companies list April 1st as the final day to save). So, we’re still highlighting the best that the […]

item.title: April Fools’ Day 2026: the best and cringiest pranks
item.description: Welcome to the worst day on the internet! As Chaim Gartenberg pointed out years ago, brands and a holiday dedicated to hoaxes are rarely a winning combo. If you’re a company with any kind of social media, internet, or AI chatbot presence in 2026, you really, truly only have four options on April Fools’ Day: […]

item.title: À cause de la crise, le Raspberry Pi augmente encore son prix
item.description: Le petit PC Raspberry Pi, autrefois connu pour son prix cassé, continue de subir l'inflation de la RAM.

item.title: The best Amazon Big Spring Sale deals you can still get
item.description: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale is technically over, but not all of the deals are gone. In fact, dozens of deals are still here and selling at the same prices, while other discounts aren’t quite as low as before. If you didn’t partake in the past week of savings, some companies list today as the final […]

item.title: How the Artemis 2 Moon Mission Could Go Dangerously Wrong
item.description: The agency has a lot riding on this historic mission—literally and figuratively. Let’s hope it doesn’t end in catastrophe.

item.title: The Korg Handytraxx Play finally got me learning to scratch
item.description: About 20 years ago, I bought a turntable with the idea that it would be a staple of my music-making setup. I dreamed of learning to scratch and digging through dollar bin vinyl for samples. Instead, it just got hooked up to a stereo. But a few months ago, I got my hands on the […]

item.title: RCS: The new reign of business messaging
item.description: For 2026 strategy, brands and technical teams can’t ignore RCS versus SMS.

item.title: The Getaway is Subaru's first three-row electric SUV
item.description: Today at the New York International Auto Show, Subaru announced its first three-row electric SUV: the Getaway. Like Subaru's other EVs, the Getaway is based on Toyota's e-TNGA platform and it shares many features and specs with the 2027 Highlander EV. Initially, all models will come with the company's Symmetrical all-wheel drive system, 420 horsepower and a native NACS-compatible charging port. That said, charging speeds don't look especially impressive as the Getaway's estimated 150kW system is expected to refill its battery from 10 to 80 percent in around 30 minutes. The first batch of Getaw...

item.title: Google Photos modifie en douce quelques une de ses fonctions « magiques »
item.description: Google remanie discrètement le mode d'accès aux fonctions d'édition « magiques » de son application Google Photos. Ces changements, pour l'instant réservés à la version Android de l'appli, mettent de côté l'approche gestuelle que l'on connaissait, pour des interactions plus « traditionnelles ».

item.title: Why NIST’s AI agent standards initiative is a turning point for enterprise security
item.description: The launch of NIST’s AI Agent Standards Initiative marks a pivotal moment in AI cybersecurity.

item.title: 'Hundreds of thousands of stolen secrets could potentially be circulating as a result of these recent attacks': Google says North Korean hackers behind major attack on Axios
item.description: North Korean hackers used an updated version of a known backdoor to target a popular npm package.

item.title: Test du M8 Mini de GMKtec : pas loin d’être le plus riquiqui des mini-PC
item.description: Soucieuse de se démarquer de certains concurrents comme Beelink, Geekom ou NiPoGi, la marque GMKtec a toujours retenu un design distinctif pour ses mini-PC. Le M8 Mini ne déroge pas à la règle et impressionne par son format qui ne doit cependant pas le mettre en difficulté : le Ryzen 5 Pro 6650H d’AMD lui donne un potentiel intéressant. Sur le papier… ...

item.title: ‘Please!’ A judge’s sassy ruling halts Trump’s White House ballroom plans
item.description: With Trump-like bombast, sarcasm, and 18 exclamation points, this court ruling brings the ballroom to a standstill. With a portion of the White House demolished and site preparation underway, President Donald Trump’s planned ballroom extension on the White House grounds has been ordered to halt construction.

item.title: This upcoming retro handheld looks like the PSP successor I've been waiting for
item.description: Evercade has revealed it's next handheld, the Nexus.

item.title: Risque d’incendie : 200 000 Peugeot et Citroën rappelées en France
item.description: Stellantis traverse une nouvelle zone de turbulences. Le groupe automobile vient de lancer une gigantesque campagne de rappel touchant près de 700 000 véhicules à travers le monde, dont environ 212 000 rien qu'en France. Cette décision fait suite à la découverte d'un défaut de conception sur des motorisations hybrides récentes pouvant déclencher un départ de feu sous le capot....

item.title: Inside Anthropic’s biggest design choices
item.description: On the latest episode of the ‘By Design’ podcast, Joel Lewenstein, Anthropic’s head of design, shares how he thinks about the role of design (and designers) in a rapidly changing landscape. Anthropic’s Claude chatbot has opinions of its own, and it’s not afraid to share them.

item.title: Quel iPad choisir en 2026 ? Voici les meilleures tablettes d’Apple
item.description: Que vous soyez un pro de la création, étudiant(e) ou simplement à la recherche d’une tablette pour Netflix et un peu de surf sur le web, voici notre sélection des meilleurs iPad sur le marché.

item.title: Best MacBooks (2026): Neo, Air, or Pro?
item.description: With the MacBook Neo in the mix, there have never been more options to choose from in Apple's MacBook lineup.

item.title: ‘Vanity metrics’ are jeopardizing AI ROI
item.description: Impressive AI stats mean nothing if they aren't tied to real business outcomes.

item.title: Lego Is Bringing Bionicle Back, But Not in the Way You’d Want
item.description: For the first time in earnest since 2016, the brick maker is putting Bionicle back on shelves... as part of the latest set of collectible minifigures.

item.title: A YouTuber channeled his distaste for the PS5’s design into slick console covers
item.description: The PlayStation 5's curvy design is divisive. The Verge's Andrew Webster called it a "robotic clam" in his 2020 review, which is actually kind of endearing. Personally, I kind of got over hating it sometime during the past almost-six years of its existence. Small form factor PC enthusiast Devyn Johnston is someone who did not […]

item.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

item.title: iPhone 17e vs Google Pixel 10a: which cheap phone makes more sense for you?
item.description: We compare Apple's entry-level iPhone 17e against Google's budget-friendly Pixel 10a.

item.title: China is moving faster on next-gen tech. The U.S. is trying to keep up
item.description: As breakthroughs in AI, brain-computer interfaces, and aviation accelerate, the real competition is over how much risk each country is willing to take. The U.S. and China are racing to define the future of technology, with very different ideas about how fast it should arrive and how tightly it should be controlled.

item.title: The end of 'shadow AI' at enterprises? Kilo launches KiloClaw for Organizations to enable secure AI agents at scale
item.description: As generative AI matures from a novelty into a workplace staple, a new friction point has emerged: the "shadow AI" or "Bring Your Own AI (BYOAI)" crisis. Much like the unsanctioned use of personal devices in years past, developers and knowledge workers are increasingly deploying autonomous agents on personal infrastructure to manage their professional workflows."Our journey with Kilo Claw has been to make it easier and easier and more accessible to folks," says Kilo co-founder Scott Breitenother. Today, the company dedicated to providing a portable, multi-model, cloud-based AI coding environme...

item.title: Apple adds the Apple TV HD to its ‘obsolete’ list despite it running tvOS 26 — and now users want a new 4K model more than ever
item.description: The Apple TV HD is now 'obsolete', even though it's working perfectly fine for happy users.

item.title: Why is OpenClaw so popular in China?
item.description: OpenClaw has gone viral in China in a way it hasn't elsewhere. Government subsidies, cheap local AI, and widespread job anxiety all help explain why.

item.title: Richard Branson Is Still Trying to Make Space Tourism Happen, Selling Tickets at $750,000
item.description: Virgin Galactic is set to resume commercial spaceflights following a two-year hiatus.

item.title: Global gas prices are still going up. Here’s why these stopgap measures aren’t enough to halt them
item.description: Experts say current workarounds aren’t adding enough oil to replace what’s stranded in the Persian Gulf. Global leaders have been scrambling to contain the rising cost of oil and gasoline since the start of the Iran war, which took a record amount of oil off the market when tankers full of crude were stranded in the Persian Gulf and military strikes damaged refineries, pipelines and export terminals.Hoping to ease some pain for consumers, President Donald Trump and other heads of state have been pulling on various levers, launching more oil on the market in a bid to calm the chaos.A group of 3...

item.title: Lucid Motors recalls over 4,000 Gravity SUVs citing improperly welded seat belts
item.description: The company struggled with quality issues when Gravity production started last year, and the new recall shows it's not out of the woods yet.

item.title: Russian iPhone users lose Apple ID payments as Kremlin escalates VPN crackdown
item.description: Russia's Ministry of Digital Development has ordered mobile operators to disable Apple ID payments in a bid to make accessing VPNs harder.

item.title: Parnasse : c’est quoi ce forfait mobile à 1 000 € par mois auquel s’attaque Free Max ?
item.description: Xavier Niel a comparé le nouveau forfait mobile Free Max à un autre proposé à plus de 1 000 euros par mois : Parnasse Monde. Voici ce qui se cache derrière ce nom méconnu.

item.title: The new Storm Radar app is a treasure trove of data for weather nerds
item.description: The horrible winter of 2026 is behind us in New England; now we’ve moved on to the season where there’s a threat of rain basically every day. Given that, the updated Storm Radar app from The Weather Company (owners of the Weather Channel app) caught my eye. There are tons of good weather apps out there, and I’m the kind of nerd that likes to try them all, and Storm Radar feels pretty unique to me. The main interface is, as you’d expect, your local radar. Tapping on any point of the map calls up a detailed forecast for that exact point, with data coming via The Weather Company’s “gridded foreca...

item.title: Nvidia et AMD sur Mac : le retour tant attendu est enfin là
item.description: Vous vous souvenez de l'époque où Apple et nvidia se faisaient la guerre ? Cette ère touche peut-être à sa fin, mais pas grâce à Cupertino. Le framework tinygrad propose désormais un pilote fonctionnel pour brancher vos GPU externes en USB4 sur Mac.

item.title: La plus puissante des clés HDMI d’Amazon revient avec une très belle remise : -40 % pour le Fire TV Stick 4K Max
item.description: Avec le Fire TV Stick 4K Max, vous avez la plus puissante des clés HDMI d'Amazon fonctionnant sous Fire TV. Ce modèle n'est pas le seul en promotion, mais clairement le plus intéressant si vous n'êtes pas satisfait de l'OS de votre TV ou de votre box TV.

item.title: Nintendo is weathering the storm
item.description: Things aren't going so well in the video game industry. Consoles are getting more expensive, games are shutting down, layoffs are prevalent, the live-service space is a mess, major platforms are entering eras of uncertainty, and AI is infiltrating the medium in frustrating ways. Finding a bright spot is rare. But amid all of that, […]

item.title: 'We are growing revenue four times faster than the companies who defined the Internet and mobile eras': OpenAI says it's making $2 billion a month — mostly from enterprise users
item.description: OpenAI is currently making $2 billion in monthly revenue — that's $6 billion quarterly or $24 billion annually.

item.title: Kia shows off the 2027 EV3 at the NY Auto Show
item.description: A couple of years after its overseas debut, Kia’s EV3 will finally come to the US. The automaker said at the New York International Auto Show that the 2027 edition of the subcompact electric SUV will arrive stateside later this year. Kia won't announce pricing until closer to its sale date, but it has previously said it would target a starting price of $35,000.The EV3 could be a welcome addition for Americans shopping for a smaller electric SUV. Compact ones that won't break the bank are a relative scarcity in the US, especially after Volvo killed the EX30. The EV3 is close to a 1:1 match with...

item.title: Guide 2026 : quels iPhone valent le coup (et lesquels sont à éviter) ?
item.description: Faut-il acheter le dernier iPhone ou bien opter pour un modèle plus ancien ? C'est à cette question que nous allons tenter de répondre dans ce guide d'achat. Quel iPhone choisir ? Voici les conseils et recommandations de la rédaction de Frandroid.

item.title: Today is the final day to save up to $150 on a PS5 before the price goes up
item.description: The cost of the PlayStation Portal and every PlayStation 5 console is going up tomorrow, April 2nd, by as much as $150 in some cases. That means time is running out if you want to save yourself the money you’d soon have to spend to secure a PS5, which is why I’m going to point […]
